content.problem: Confusion between ~/.gemini/ and ~/.agent/ as single source of truth - both contained comprehensive content with different update patterns
content.discovery: Found ~/.gemini contains 22 items, ~/.agent contains 9 items, ~/.gemini was not a symlink to ~/.agent but a separate directory with richer content
content.solution: Establish ~/.agent/ as single source of truth by migrating unique content from ~/.gemini/ and updating symlinks appropriately
improvements_implemented: Created 5 automated validation systems (symlink health, skill discovery, version consistency, config management, reference integrity)
Implemented dynamic skill discovery replacing hardcoded paths
Established centralized configuration management with multi-source loading
Created cross-reference integrity checking with automatic fix capabilities
Updated documentation to reflect correct single source of truth location
Fixed naming convention inconsistencies across all documentation
lessons_learned: Directory architecture needs clear documentation to prevent confusion
Automated systems are essential for cross-agent compatibility
Content analysis should drive structural decisions
Symlinks solve sandbox limitations but require health monitoring
Migration should be systematic and reversible
Single source of truth must be directory with most comprehensive content
next_steps: Complete migration of unique content from ~/.gemini/ to ~/.agent/
Integrate validation scripts into mandatory RTB workflow
Create continuous monitoring for structural health
Document architecture decisions for future reference
